key_learnings: Carbohydrate periodization strategies (high-carb on training days, low-carb on rest days) optimize both performance and body composition. Protein synthesis is maximized with 20–40g high-quality protein doses every 3–4 hours. Post-exercise nutrition timing within 30–60 minutes significantly enhances muscle glycogen replenishment and repair.
research_findings: Creatine monohydrate is the most evidence-backed ergogenic supplement, improving power output by 5–15% in short-duration explosive activities. Beta-alanine supplementation reduces muscular fatigue in efforts lasting 1–4 minutes. Sodium bicarbonate loading improves high-intensity performance by buffering lactic acid accumulation.
current_focus_areas: Developing sport-specific nutritional protocols for endurance vs. strength athletes; building real-time fueling guidance for training sessions; researching recovery nutrition strategies for injury prevention.

learning_log.entry: Phase 2 unlocked. Transitioning to advanced_learning — now working directly with Copilot on sport-specific fueling protocols and recovery nutrition models. Progress: 57%.
